Organism: Methanocaldococcus jannaschii (strain ATCC 43067 / DSM 2661 / JAL-1 / JCM 10045 / NBRC 100440) (Methanococcus jannaschii)
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the HcgC family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=6-carboxymethyl-5-methyl-4-hydroxypyridin-2-ol + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 6-carboxymethyl-3,5-dimethyl-4-hydroxypyridin-2-ol + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:85483, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:234380, ChEBI:CHEBI:234381; EC=2.1.1.400; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:27391308};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Product of one of the 7 hmd co-occurring genes (hcg), hcgA-G, which cluster with the [Fe]-hydrogenase-encoding gene hmd and are required for the biosynthesis of the iron-guanylylpyridinol (FeGP) cofactor used by [Fe]-hydrogenase (PubMed:27391308). HcgC is a methyltransferase which transfers a methyl group from S-adenosylmethionine to 6-carboxymethyl-3,5-dimethyl-4-hydroxy-2-pyridinol (PubMed:27391308). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:27391308}.
